channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court rejects appeal from Texas officer convicted in killing of woman through her window
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court won't hear an appeal from a former Texas police officer convicted in the death of a woman who was shot through a window of her home. Aaron Dean was convicted of manslaughter in Atatiana Jefferson's fatal shooting, and he was sentenced to nearly 12 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Republican-led challenge to ease voter registration
item.description: The Supreme Court has turned away a challenge from Republican state lawmakers in Pennsylvania to a Biden administration executive order that is intended to boost voter registration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court lets stand a decision barring emergency abortions that violate Texas ban
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday let stand a decision barring emergency abortions that violate the law in Texas, which has one of the country's strictest abortion bans.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Deputy Mayor for Public Safety Philip Banks resigns
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Deputy Mayor for Public Safety Philip Banks III has resigned from his position, his spokespeople said on Monday. The resignation comes after sources said Friday that First Deputy Mayor Sheena Wright was also expected to resign.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: GOP New Jersey Senate candidate Curtis Bashaw nearly passes out during debate
item.description: New Jersey Republican Senate candidate Curtis Bashaw appeared to freeze on Sunday during his debate against Democrat opponent Rep. Andy Kim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One-year anniversary of Oct. 7 attacks arrives with lasting trauma for Israelis, American Jews: expert
item.description: Israelis are displaced and still coping with the trauma brought by the Oct. 7 terrorist attacks one year later, as Jewish American life remains altered.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Republicans mark Oct 7 attack 1 year out as Israel-Hamas war continues
item.description: Senate Republicans issued a renewed condemnation of the Oct. 7, 2023, terrorist attacks perpetrated by Hamas in Israel one year after they occurred.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Battleground Senate candidate unloads on 'radical' Dem opponent for disparaging Trump voters
item.description: Arizona GOP Senate candidate Kari Lake spoke to Fox News Digital about why she believes her opponent, Rep. Ruben Gallego, is not a "moderate" candidate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's plan would add $4 trillion more to U.S. debt compared to Harris', analysis says
item.description: Neither former President Donald Trump nor Vice President Kamala Harris have plans to fix the national debt, risking "an eventual fiscal crisis," a budget nonprofit group found in its analysis.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: GOP looks to avoid disaster in Nebraska Senate race
item.description: Republicans are looking to avoid a disaster in the Nebraska Senate race amid signs that an independent candidate could be giving Sen. Deb Fischer (R-Neb.) a run for her money.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk makes his first appearance at a Trump rally and casts the election in dire terms
item.description: Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during an appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump returns to rally crowd in Butler, Pa., site of July assassination attempt
item.description: The former President spoke at a campaign rally in the same location that a bullet grazed his ear in July. The shooter killed a man and injured two other people.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman, MPR News

item.title: Harris pledges ongoing federal support as she visits North Carolina to survey Helene's aftermath
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris pledged ongoing federal support and praised the “heroes among us” as she visited North Carolina on Saturday in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, her second trip in four days to the disaster zone.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California Swing Districts: Rep. Steel, Tran make their case to voters in CA-45
item.description: (Inside California Politics) - Inside California Politics is talking to the candidates in various swing districts across California that could impact the balance of power in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Inside California Politics, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Milton projected to reach Category 4 strength before landfall in Florida
item.description: A new storm is approaching the west coast of the Florida, threatening to inflict heavy rain, strong winds and flash flooding in the already-storm battered state.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: The Latest: Harris to visit Michigan while Trump heads to Georgia
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris will visit the union stronghold of Flint, Michigan, on Friday as she battles with Donald Trump for working-class voters who could tip the scales in this year’s election. Her appearance in the battleground state comes the day after U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Newsom awards $131M to clear homeless encampments; Los Angeles gets $11.3M
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom announced Friday that the state will grant 18 cities $131 million to clear homeless encampments and provide shelter, care and support. The grants are a part of the state’s $1 billion Encampment Resolution Funds. The city of Los Angeles is slated to receive $11.3 million in funding.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Civil rights groups ask to extend voter registration deadlines in hurricane-ravaged states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With registration deadlines looming, Democrats and civil rights groups are asking election officials in the states ravaged by Hurricane Helene to give voters more time. A judge in South Carolina on Friday extended that state's deadline to Oct.
item.dc:creator: AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Craig, Teirab tangle over Social Security, abortion in first head-to-head 2nd District debate
item.description: In a policy driven debate, candidates in Minnesota’s 2nd Congressional District part on fiscal and abortion policy while finding common ground on response to Jan. 6 Capitol uprising.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth and Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Obama is set to hit the campaign trail for Vice President Harris this month, starting his blitz in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania next week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court declines to block Biden limits on methane, toxic pollution
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday declined to block Biden administration limits on planet-warming methane from oil and gas production and toxic pollution from coal plants.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Eric Adams's future in question as legal woes threaten to mount
item.description: Embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams is fighting for his political life as he finds himself in the middle of a legal firestorm, raising questions about how much longer he can hold on to power. Adams was indicted last week by the Manhattan U.S.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: New Yorkers think Eric Adams should resign after corruption indictment: poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Nearly 70% of New York City residents think Mayor Eric Adams should resign following his corruption indictment, according to a Marist College poll released Friday. Another 63% of New Yorkers think Gov. Kathy Hochul should remove Adams from his position if he does not resign.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: When is the voter register deadline in New York and New Jersey?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, PIX 11

item.title: Firefighters union won't endorse in Trump-Harris race
item.description: The International Association of Fire Fighters (IAFF) announced Thursday it would not endorse a candidate in this year's presidential race, becoming the second major union to do so in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Debate performance gives Vance 2028 White House boost
item.description: Sen. JD Vance’s (R-Ohio) debate performance is giving him a shot in the arm to become a leading 2028 presidential contender no matter what happens in November between former President Trump and Vice President Harris.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Programming alert: NewsNation to feature PA Senate race tonight
item.description: NewsNation will have live coverage of the Pennsylvania senate debate between Bob Casey and David McCormick.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Politically charged rumors and conspiracy theories about Helene flourish on X
item.description: False claims about the hurricane and the government’s response got millions of views on social media. Emergency management researchers say it makes it harder for useful information to surface.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan, MPR News

item.title: NYC Schools Chancellor Banks to step down sooner than expected
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Schools Chancellor David Banks is expected to step down from his position sooner than expected, several months before he was supposed to retire, a City Hall spokesperson confirmed to PIX11 News on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump calls new January 6th accusations 'pure election interference'
item.description: The former president spoke exclusively to NewsNation about the unsealing of documents related to his election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, Fox 5

item.title: Ranked choice voting is on the ballot in several states
item.description: Ranked choice voting could be coming to states like Colorado, Nevada and Oregon. Here's what to know.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris voiced her support Wednesday for striking port workers while bashing her rival former President Donald Trump after tens of thousands of longshoremen at ports along the East Coast and Gulf of Mexico walked off the job.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: What we gleaned from key moments of the Walz-Vance debate in New York
item.description: MPR News senior politics reporter Dana Ferguson is in New York with takeaways from the only debate between the vice presidential hopefuls.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Dana Ferguson, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: What to know about the Walz-Vance debate
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) and Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) are set to go head-to-head on Tuesday night in their first and likely only debate as vice presidential contenders.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5
